quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: The Strait of Juan de Fuca, which you can see from Port Angeles, was named after a Greek sailor who sailed under a Spanish name! Ioannis Phokas (Juan de Fuca) claimed to have discovered this waterway in 1592, though historians still debate whether he actually sailed these waters. The strait connects the Pacific Ocean to Puget Sound and is home to orcas, seals, and gray whales.

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Hummingbirds
short_read.teaser: These tiny jewels of the bird world have hearts that beat over 1,200 times per minute and can remember every flower they've visited. Discover the amazing abilities that make hummingbirds true marvels of nature.

medium_read.title: The Joy of Growing Herbs in Small Spaces
medium_read.teaser: Even a sunny windowsill can become a thriving herb garden that brings fresh flavors and wonderful scents to your home. This guide shows how easy it is to grow basil, rosemary, and other favorites indoors year-round.

long_read.title: The Enduring Legacy of Hungarian Folk Art
long_read.teaser: From intricate embroidery to vibrant pottery, Hungarian folk traditions have been passed down through generations, preserving stories and symbols that connect communities. This beautiful exploration celebrates the artisans keeping these traditions alive today.

port_angeles.title: Hurricane Ridge Wildflower Season
port_angeles.description: Early June marks the beginning of wildflower season at Hurricane Ridge in Olympic National Park! As the snow melts, alpine meadows burst with color from lupines, Indian paintbrush, and avalanche lilies. The views of the Olympic Mountains and the Strait are breathtaking this time of year.

puzzle_1.question: A single hummingbird may visit up to ___ flowers in a single day to get enough nectar for energy.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Exactly right! Those tiny birds are incredibly busy, visiting thousands of flowers every day!
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! Hummingbirds actually visit around 2,000 flowers each day — they're amazing little workers!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: It's a surprisingly high number — about 2,000 flowers a day! These tiny birds need enormous amounts of energy to fuel their rapid wing beats.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: Hummingbirds have the highest metabolism of all animals, which is why they need to eat so frequently. They consume about half their body weight in sugar daily and can visit over 1,000 flowers per day just to survive.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderful! Pillangó is indeed butterfly — such a lovely, light word that seems to flutter off the tongue!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The word is pillangó — doesn't it sound as delicate as a butterfly itself? (Virág means flower, madár means bird, and napsugár means sunbeam.)
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is a unique language, not related to most European languages but part of the Finno-Ugric family. Many Hungarian words are beautifully descriptive, often capturing the essence of what they describe.

puzzle_3.question: Olympic National Park is one of the wettest places in the continental United States. Which temperate ecosystem thrives there because of this rainfall?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Perfect! The Hoh Rain Forest and other temperate rainforests in Olympic National Park receive over 140 inches of rain annually, creating magical forests draped in moss and ferns!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: It's the temperate rainforest! Places like the Hoh Rain Forest get over 140 inches of rain each year, creating those enchanting green forests covered in moss that make the Olympic Peninsula so special.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Olympic National Park contains one of the largest intact temperate rainforests in the world. The massive rainfall supports enormous Sitka spruce and western hemlock trees, some over 300 feet tall, draped in club moss and ferns.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
